system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,omega)  Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Face 0 in patch 0 does not have neighbour cell

**Error Message:**
```
face 0 in patch 0 does not have neighbour cell face: 4(4 5 14 13)
```

### Diagnosis

This error occurs because the `blockMeshDict` references **vertex indices that don't exist**. The current `blockMeshDict` defines only **12 vertices** (indices 0 through 11), but the boundary definitions reference vertices like `13`, `14`, etc. These are out-of-bounds indices.

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`:

- Only 12 vertices are defined (indices 0-11)
- But the boundary patches reference vertices up to index 15+

The root cause is that the vertex numbering scheme assigns **multiple logical vertices to the same physical position**. For example, vertices 0 and 4 both represent `(0, 0, -0.5)`, vertices 1 and 5 both represent `(0, 25.4, -0.5)`, and so on. This duplication means the vertex indices used in the boundary face definitions don't correspond to the actual vertex list.

In the previous attempt, the suggestion was to remove the duplicate vertices at z=206 and z=-206, but the current `blockMeshDict` still has duplicated vertices at z=-0.5 and z=0.5 across multiple x-segments.

### Solution

The `blockMeshDict` needs to be rewritten with a **consistent vertex indexing scheme** where each physical corner of the mesh has exactly one vertex entry. For a 2D channel with vertices at x = {0, 206}, y = {0, 25.4}, z = {-0.5, 0.5}, there should be only **8 unique vertices**:

```
vertices
(
    (-20.6 0 -0.5)    (-20.6 25.4 -0.5)    (0 -25.4 -0.5)    (0 0 -0.5)
    (-20.6 0 0.5)     (-20.6 25.4 0.5)     (0 -25.4 0.5)     (0 0 0.5)
    (206 -25.4 -0.5)  (206 0 -0.5)         (206 -25.4 0.5)   (206 0 0.5)
    (206 25.4 -0.5)   (206 25.4 0.5)
);
```

But wait — the geometry shows the channel runs from x=0 to x=206, with the inlet at x=-20.6. So the correct vertex set should cover x = {-20.6, 0, 206}. That gives us 3 × 2 × 2 = 12 unique vertices. The issue is that the **block connectivity** needs to be recalculated based on this 12-vertex layout.

With 12 unique vertices, the blocks should connect them properly. The two blocks are:
- Left/lower block: connects vertices at x={-20.6, 0}
- Right/upper block: connects vertices at x={0, 206}

Each block is a hex with 8 vertices. The correct block definitions should be:

```
blocks
(
    hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) (18 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11) (18 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);
```

Wait — checking the vertex coordinates again, vertices 4 and 5 are at x=0, and vertices 8 and 9 are also at x=0... No, looking more carefully at the current vertex list, vertices 4 and 5 are at x=0, but vertices 8 and 9 are at x=206. The blocks should be:

First block (spanning x=-20.6 to x=0): `hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)` — bottom face 0→3→2→1, top face 4→7→6→5
Second block (spanning x=0 to x=206): `hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9)` — bottom face 4→7→6→5, top face 8→11→10→9

Now check the boundary faces against the actual vertex count. The inlet face `(4 5 14 13)` references vertices 13 and 14 which don't exist. The boundary face definitions need to be recomputed based on the correct block vertex ordering.

The inlet face comes from the first block's bottom face. After reordering `hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)`, the bottom face is `(0 3 2 1)` and the top face is `(4 7 6 5)`. The inlet boundary face should trace around the left side of the first block — likely `(4 5 14 13)` was computed from an old vertex layout. With the new correct layout, the inlet face should be something like `(4 5 14 13)` only if those vertices exist. Actually, tracing the left face of block 0: vertices 0, 4 on the left side. The inlet face should be `(0 4 7 3)` — but that's already assigned to `lowerWall`.

Rewriting the entire `blockMeshDict` with 12 unique vertices and correctly indexed boundary faces is essential. Every face in every boundary patch must reference valid vertex indices from the 0-11 range.

## Error 2: `log.pimpleFoam` — Cascading error

This is purely a cascading error — since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh exists, so `pimpleFoam` cannot find the `points` file. This resolves automatically once `blockMesh` succeeds.

## Summary

1. **Rewrite `blockMeshDict`**: Define exactly 12 unique vertices (no duplicates). Recalculate all block definitions and boundary face vertex indices based on the correct vertex layout. Ensure all face definitions use only vertex indices 0 through 11. Pay special attention to computing the correct face vertex orderings for `inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, and `frontAndBack` based on the final block topology.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-05.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace all vertices with 12 unique vertices: v0=(-20.6 0 -0.5), v1=(-20.6 25.4 -0.5), v2=(0 -25.4 -0.5), v3=(0 0 -0.5), v4=(-20.6 0 0.5), v5=(-20.6 25.4 0.5), v6=(0 -25.4 0.5), v7=(0 0 0.5), v8=(206 -25.4 -0.5), v9=(206 0 -0.5), v10=(206 -25.4 0.5), v11=(206 0 0.5), v12=(206 25.4 -0.5), v13=(206 25.4 0.5); Replace blocks with hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (18 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1) and hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9) (18 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Fix inlet patch faces to (0 4 5 1) instead of (4 5 14 13); Fix outlet patch faces to (2 3 7 6) instead of (2 3 15 12); Fix upperWall patch faces to (1 5 9 13) and (3 2 6 10) instead of (1 3 11 10) and (5 6 15 14); Fix lowerWall patch faces to (0 3 7 4) and (6 10 11 8) instead of (0 4 7 3) and (6 11 10 9); Fix frontAndBack patch faces to (0 1 5 4) (4 5 9 8) (3 2 6 7) (7 6 10 11) -- actually these need to map to correct vertex indices from the new layout; Rewrite entire file with proper 14 vertices and correct face indexing')]
